    1. handle(MethodExceptionContext)

Interface MethodExceptionPolicy

All Known Implementing Classes:
NeverRethrowMethodExceptionPolicy, RethrowIfNoRecoveryMethodExceptionPolicy
Functional Interface:
This is a functional interface and can therefore be used as the assignment target for a lambda expression or method reference.
@FunctionalInterface public interface MethodExceptionPolicy
Strategy for deciding the final outcome after a primary method call fails and failover recovery has been attempted.

Implementations receive a MethodExceptionContext containing the original exception, the recovered result (if any), and the full call context. They may:

  • return context.recoveredResult() — serve stale data transparently
  • return null — propagate nothing to the caller
  • rethrow context.cause() — cascade the original exception to the caller

Built-in implementations:

  • MethodExceptionHandler — default; returns recovered result or null, never throws
  • RethrowIfNoRecoveryMethodExceptionPolicy — returns recovered result if present, otherwise rethrows

Custom policies can be registered as a Spring bean and will be picked up automatically by the auto-configuration.

Author:
Anand Manissery

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    <T> T
    handle(MethodExceptionContext<T> context)
    Handles the exception context and returns the value to return to the caller, or throws to propagate the failure.

  • Method Details

    • handle

      <T> T handle(MethodExceptionContext<T> context)
      Handles the exception context and returns the value to return to the caller, or throws to propagate the failure.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the return type of the intercepted method
      Parameters:
      context - all available information about the failure and recovery attempt
      Returns:
      the value to return to the caller; may be null
